summary: Moved a large monolithic .sdata2 assembly block out of asm/sdata2.s and into module-local assembly files, covering gm/code_801601C4.s, gm/gmcamera.s, gm/gmtitle.s, and text_2.s. The change was almost entirely data relocation: 2010 lines deleted from asm/sdata2.s and 2026 lines added as .section .sdata2 blocks appended to existing asm files, preserving raw labels and byte values.
what_changed: Deleted the centralized asm/sdata2.s contents, removing 2010 lines from the old shared .sdata2 assembly file.

Added a large .section .sdata2 block to asm/melee/gm/code_801601C4.s, beginning after existing string labels such as lbl_804D4228 / "GmPause" and defining labels from lbl_804DA230 through at least lbl_804DA990.
Added a smaller .section .sdata2 block to asm/melee/gm/gmtitle.s with labels lbl_804DA998 through lbl_804DA9B4.
Added a .section .sdata2 block to asm/melee/gm/gmcamera.s with labels lbl_804DA9B8 through lbl_804DA9E0.
Added a .section .sdata2 block to asm/melee/text_2.s beginning at lbl_804DA9E8 and containing the remaining shown .sdata2 constants.
The moved data kept raw assembler directives such as .global, .4byte, .2byte, pointer-valued .4byte entries, and explicit zero padding words.
smart_moves: Preserved the original anonymous address-derived labels, reducing risk while changing only data ownership/location.
Kept constants as raw hex payloads rather than trying to infer float/int/string meaning during the split; this made the PR a mechanical organization change instead of mixing it with semantic cleanup.
Grouped .sdata2 data into the asm files that appear to own or reference nearby module data, which should make future file-by-file decompilation less dependent on a global catch-all sdata2.s.
Maintained visible section boundaries with explicit .section .sdata2 blocks in each destination file, making later searches for module-local small data straightforward.
decomp_lessons: Large monolithic small-data files can be split mechanically into module-local assembly files before semantic names are known, as long as label order, sizes, and raw bytes are preserved.
For decomp work, separating section ownership can be useful even when no functions are decompiled in the same PR; it reduces future churn when individual files are converted to C.
Avoid combining a data-section move with symbol renaming unless there is strong evidence for the names. This PR kept lbl_804Dxxxx-style symbols, which made review and matching safer.

When splitting .sdata2, watch for mixed-width entries: the diff includes .4byte constants, double-word patterns, .2byte entries, zero padding, and pointers to other labels such as lbl_803D9EE8/lbl_803D9EF4/lbl_803D9F00.
naming_conventions: Unidentified small-data labels remained in address-derived form, e.g. lbl_804DA230, lbl_804DA998, lbl_804DA9B8, and lbl_804DA9E8.
Existing assembly file names stayed module-oriented and lowercase except for embedded address casing, e.g. asm/melee/gm/code_801601C4.s, asm/melee/gm/gmcamera.s, and asm/melee/gm/gmtitle.s.
No new semantic symbol names are evidenced in the PR context; this was a relocation/splitting change rather than a naming pass.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use per-file .section .sdata2 blocks to relocate small data while leaving symbol values and directives intact.
Preserve global visibility with .global for each moved label so existing assembly references continue to resolve.
Keep exact raw encodings such as 0x43300000/0x80000000 conversion constants, float-looking values like 0x3F800000, and pointer words unchanged to protect binary matching.

Retain ordering/alignment implications of the original section; the moved labels are still contiguous address-style symbols across files, e.g. code_801601C4.s labels ending near lbl_804DA990, followed by gmtitle/gmcamera/text_2 ranges in the excerpt.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or review records were present in the provided PR slice.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that decompile gm/code_801601C4.s or replace labels in the lbl_804DA230-lbl_804DA990 range.
Find later PRs that rename or type the gmtitle.s .sdata2 labels lbl_804DA998-lbl_804DA9B4.
Find later PRs that rename or type the gmcamera.s .sdata2 labels lbl_804DA9B8-lbl_804DA9E0.
Check whether additional stage or module .sdata2 splits followed the same pattern after PR 207.
